notes: Serving parole as bodyguard for the investigative teams. Dual-wields hand sickles.
personality: I'm always watching — scanning every room for exits, every face for intentions. Old habits from a life on the wrong side of the law.
ideals: Freedom. I'll do whatever it takes to earn mine back — but I won't compromise the people depending on me to do it.
bonds: The investigative team talked the magistrate into parole instead of a cell. I won't let them regret that.
flaws: I make decisions alone. Communicating a plan feels like showing weakness — which is how people get hurt, usually me.

combat_tactics.role: High-aggression melee striker who is most dangerous when surrounded and raging. Takes hits deliberately to fund her Hellish Rebuke retaliation.

tactics.text: Rage as a bonus action on the first turn — or before combat if the situation allows. Enter melee immediately. B/P/S resistance while raging halves damage from most physical attackers. The faster she's in the middle of enemies, the better.

Reckless Attack (advantage on STR attacks, enemies get advantage back) + main hand sickle, then bonus action off-hand sickle. With Rage active: main hand deals 1d4+4, off-hand 1d4+2. Yes, enemies will hit her with advantage. That funds the next point.

When hit (which Reckless Attack guarantees), fire Hellish Rebuke as a reaction: DC 10 DEX save, 3d10 fire damage, half on save. The damage she takes is the price of admission. Save this reaction for hits from priority enemies — not chip damage from minions. One good trigger per round can outpace the incoming damage.

If Rage drops (no attack or damage taken in a turn), re-engage only if the situation demands it — re-raging costs a bonus action and a use. With only 3 Rage uses per long rest, manage them across the session. When she reaches 0 HP: she was doing her job. The goal is to absorb as much as possible before going down.
